original: Ballon d'Or: Ajax forwardJohan Cruyffwins back-to-back awards, and his 3rd overall, for best European football player ahead of Bayern Munich defender Franz Beckenbauer and Legia Warsaw midfielder Kazimierz Deyna
South African & US surgeons separate Zambian Siamese twins joined at the head

Pittsburgh NHL centerMario Lemieuxaccounts for all his team's goals in Penguins 8-6 win over the NJ Devils; famously scores 5 goals, each in a different way: even strength, power play, shorthanded, penalty shot and empty net; also 3 assists
Marie Curiereceives her second Nobel Prize, this time in Chemistry, for her work with radioactivity
A world record 22,990 mm of rain falls in a single year in Cherrapunji, Assam, India
Would have been start of Aust/Eng Test Cricket at MCG, washed out
Cartoonist Bill Watterson ends his "Calvin & Hobbes" comic strip after 10 years, believing he had achieved all he could in the medium
Evel Knievelfails in his attempt to jump the Caesar's Palace Fountain, Las Vegas, breaking his pelvis, femur, wrist, hip and both ankles
Artur Fischer, German inventor with more patents than Edison, born in Tumlingen, Baden-Württemberg (d. 2016)
Émilie Le Pennec, French gymnast (Olympic gold uneven bars 2004), born in La Garenne-Colombes, France
Ellesse Andrews, New Zeland track cyclist (Olympic gold women's sprint, keirin 2024; silver keirin 2020, team 2024), born in Christchurch, New Zealand
1st battery to convert radioactive energy to electricity announced
Popular Electronics displays Altair 8800 computer

Winterland Ballroom in San Francisco, California closes after 556 concerts; Final show is the Grateful Dead, New Riders of the Purple Sage, and The Blues Brothers; program lasts for over 8 hours, with the Grateful Dead playing for nearly 6 hours; concert broadcast on local PBS TV station KQED, and simulcast by radio station KSAN-FM, after the show, the crowd was treated to a hot, buffet-style breakfast.
Odetta [Holmes], American folk singer (Sanctuary), actress and civil rights activist, born in Birmingham, Alabama (d. 2008)
Alexander Bolshunov, Russian cross-country skier (Olympic gold 30k, 50k, 4 x 10k relay 2022; Tour de Ski 2021, 22), born in Podyvotye, Russia
1st NBA game at Great Western Forum, LA Lakers beat Houston 147-118
Test Cricket debut of Bishen Singh Bedi, India v WI Calcutta, 2-92

Buffalo's Czech NHL goalie Dominik Hasek stops 36 shots for his 27th career shutout in Sabres' 3-0 win over Ottawa Senators; his 6th shutout in month of December; first since George Hainsworth in 1928-29 to achieve the feat

A heavy, dense fog rolls over Soldier Field in Chicago during the second quarter of the Bears vs. Eagles NFC Divisional Playoff Game, cutting visibility to 15-20 yards in a game known as "The Fog Bowl" (Bears win 20-12)
Willye White, American athlete (Olympic silver long jump 1956, 4x100m relay 1964; Sports Illustrated 100 greatest women athletes of 20th century), born in Money, Mississippi (d. 2007)
Helen Richey becomes 1st woman to pilot an airmail transport
Official opening of Taipei 101, then the tallest skyscraper in the world, at a height of 509 metres (1,670 feet)
The Monkees single "I'm a Believer", written byNeil Diamond, hits #1 and stays there for 7 weeks
Connie Willis, American science fiction and fantasy author, 11x Hugo Award and 7x Nebula Award winner (Blackout, All Clear, Doomsday Book), born in Denver, Colorado
Inge Helten, German athlete (Olympic silver FRG 4x100m relay, bronze 100m 1976; WR 100m 11.04s 1976), born in Sinzig, Germany
Cornerstone laid for Honolulu's Iolani Palace (only royal palace in US)
Edison gives 1st public demonstration of his incandescent lamp
First New Year's Eve celebration held in Times Square, New York City
For 1st time, a ball drops at Times Square to signal the New Year
Charles Darrow patents the board game Monopoly, goes on to be the 1st millionaire game designer
Dr R N Harger's "drunkometer", 1st breath test, introduced in Indiana
Dutch leave New Guinea
Record high barometric pressure (1083.8 mb, 32 inches) recorded at Agata in Siberia, Soviet Union; eclipsed in December 2001
Soviet TU-144 makes its maiden flight, the first by a commercial supersonic airliner
Leap second day; also in 1973-79, 1987
Date skipped in Kiribati as the Phoenix Islands and Line Islands change time zones from UTC-11 to UTC+13 and UTC-10 to UTC+14, respectively
Ban on ivory trade in China comes into effect
WHO grants the Pfizer/BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine emergency authorization, paving the way for worldwide distribution
English astronomerJames Bradleyannounces the discovery of Earth's nutation, or wobble
Jerry Garciaand Bob Weir of The Grateful Dead play music together for the 1st time at Dana Morgan's Music Store in Palo Alto, California
Largest free concert in the world occurs whenRod Stewartperforms in front of four million people at Copacabana Beach, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
Yoriyuki Arima, Japanese mathematician (approximated the value of pi and its square, pi^2), born in Kurume, Kyūshū, Japan (d. 1783)
Bob Shaw, Northern Irish sci-fi author (Light of Other Days, The Ragged Astronauts), born in Belfast, Northern Ireland (d. 1996)
Lyudmila Pakhomova, Russian ice dancer (World C'ship gold x 6; Olympic gold USSR 1976; with Alexandr Gorshkov), born in Moscow, Russia (d. 1986)
Michael Hedges, American new-age guitarist, composer, and singer (Aerial Boundaries), born in Sacramento, California (d. 1997)
Denée Benton, American actress (Hamilton, The Gilded Age), born in Orlando, Florida
Hunter Schafer, American model, actress and LGBTQ activist, born in Trenton, New Jersey
A window tax is imposed in England, causing many shopkeepers to brick up their windows to avoid the tax.
The Beach Boys play their debut gig under that name
DDT, first of the modern insecticides used against malaria and typhus, is banned by the US Environmental Protection Agency after evidence of adverse environmental side effects
The Cars played their 1st gig
Matthew Elliott scores separate cricket century same day for Victoria
US PresidentHarry Trumanofficially proclaims end of WW II
Johan Cruyffchosen European Football Player of theYear
Avram Hershko, Hungarian-born Israeli biochemist and Nobel laureate in Chemistry, born in Karcag, Hungary
Rita Lee [Jones], Brazilian rock singer, songwriter and musician (Os Mutantes), born in São Paulo, Brazil (d. 2023)[1]
René Robert, Canadian ice hockey winger (NHL All Star 1972-73, 74-75; Buffalo Sabres 1st 100 point player 1974-75), born in Trois-Rivières, Quebec (d. 2021)
Johan Rockström, Swedish scientist (Director Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research, Planetary Boundaries guidelines), born in Finspång, Sweden
Tony Kanaan, Brazilian auto racer (Indianapolis 500 2013; IndyCar Series 2004; Indy Lights Series 1997), born in Salvador, Brazil
